req.title: System-Admin deletes onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a onboarding-task through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path deletion by an authenticated admin (EP – valid partition), (2) deletion attempt without authentication returning 401 (NEG), (3) deletion of a non-existent / already-deleted task to probe the boundary between 'exists' and 'does not exist' states (BVA/STC), (4) deletion with a forbidden role returning 403 (DT – role column), and (5) audit-record verification after successful deletion (STC – state change). Five tests satisfy the minimum of three while giving full branch coverage of the error pathways listed in the requirement.

tests.name: test_admin_delete_onboarding_task_success
preconditions: A valid onboarding-task exists with a known task_id
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token fixture>
Create a disposable onboarding-task via <create_onboarding_task helper> to guarantee isolation
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
trace: R-WEB-027 – positive outcome: task deleted, response 200/201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_delete_onboarding_task_success(admin_token, base_url, create_onboarding_task):
    task_id = create_onboarding_task(admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(task_id) in str(body), "Deleted entity id not found in response"

    verify = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}",
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert verify.status_code == 404, (
        f"Task should be gone (404) but got {verify.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_returns_401
preconditions: A valid onboarding-task exists (created via admin beforehand)

tests.name: test_delete_already_deleted_task_returns_boundary_status
preconditions: A valid onboarding-task is created and immediately deleted once
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token fixture>
Create a disposable task via <create_onboarding_task helper>
Delete the task once (first DELETE – expect 200/201)
Delete the same task again (second DELETE – boundary probe)
expected: Second DELETE returns 404 or 410 (resource no longer exists)
trace: R-WEB-027 – BVA: boundary between existing and non-existing resource state
R-WEB-027 – STC: state transition from 'active' to 'deleted' to 'not found'

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_delete_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds a non-admin role (e.g. viewer)
actions: Obtain non-admin auth token via <get_viewer_token fixture>
Send DELETE /api/onboarding-tasks/{task_id} with the non-admin token
trace: R-WEB-027 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_audit_record_written_after_delete
preconditions: Audit log API or DB helper is accessible via <get_audit_records helper>
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token fixture>
Create a disposable task via <create_onboarding_task helper>
Query the audit log for a record referencing task_id and action DELETE
expected: Audit log contains exactly one record for task_id with action 'DELETE' or 'delete'
trace: R-WEB-027 – expected outcome: audit record written on success
STC: system transitions from pre-delete to post-delete state, audit record created
